arts.ttl: Enzo Maresca speaks out on Chelsea's Cole Palmer dependency
arts.sum: Chelsea manager Enzo Maresca addressed concerns that his side are overly dependent on Cole Palmer.

arts.ttl: Marcel Desailly 'set for court date amid ongoing paternity row over 10-year-old daughter he has refused to acknowledge' which could see the Chelsea legend - who is 'struggling financially' - forced to pay £4000-a-month in alimony
arts.sum: Desailly and Cosma Batista de Alcantara are due at the Paris judicial court on Tuesday amid an ongoing case to confirm whether he is the biological father of his ex-partner's daughter.

arts.ttl: Enzo Maresca is right to be cautious but rebuilt Chelsea are in the title race | Jacob Steinberg
arts.sum: Italian’s tactical versatility has made the Blues contenders as they head to Spurs, even if he insists it is too soon Nobody takes Enzo Maresca seriously any more. The Italian keeps playing down Chelsea’s chances of muscling themselves into the title race, but the line is becoming harder to hold. Modesty, after all, does become less believable when analysing a team with the Premier League’s most productive attack, growing bite and control in midfield, an improving defence and a hunger that makes them a match for anyone. Maresca was not budging before Sunday’s trip to Tottenham. The message remains that Chelsea are ahead of expectations. Maresca’s position is that his young side are not ready to compete with Arsenal, Liverpool and, yes, Manchester City. He believes his players need to suffer more before they possess the mentality required to match the very best over an entire campaign. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Chelsea 'impressed' by Premier League star after scouting mission
arts.sum: Chelsea scouts have been left impressed by what they have seen from Bournemouth defender Ilya Zabarnyi, according to a report. Chelsea 'impressed' by Premier League star after scouting mission

arts.ttl: ‘Take them apart’: Chris Sutton has now predicted the result of Tottenham vs Chelsea
arts.sum: Tottenham host Chelsea on Sunday in what should be an absolutely brilliant game in the Premier League. Chelsea and Tottenham always have incredible battles whenever...

arts.ttl: Andre Villas Boas sends former club Chelsea FIFA Club World Cup warning
arts.sum: Andre Villas Boas has warned his former club Chelsea that the prize money for the FIFA Club World Cup is 'below initial expectations.' Chelsea and City have qualified for the Club World Cup by virtue of winning the Champions League over the course of the last four years.

arts.ttl: Tottenham informed whether 'world-class' midfield target will be leaving his club in January - Sky Sports
Tottenham could do worse than complete six-year transfer mission to sign forgotten Chelsea man - Lee Wilmot
arts.sum: But Spurs could do worse than look to a player they were linked with signing six years ago to help with their injury crisis at the other end of the field. Ben Chilwell was heavily linked with Spurs in 2018 before signing a new deal to stay at Leicester City.

arts.ttl: In search of the origins to the toxic Tottenham-Chelsea rivalry featuring meat hooks at White Hart Lane, electrified fencing at Stamford Bridge and some football in between
arts.sum: KIERAN GILL: In search of its origins, before there was even a flicker of a feud, Mail Sport was told to look back to the 1967 FA Cup final - or the 'Cockney Cup final' as it was christened.
